framework.name: OpenID Agentic Identity Whitepaper - The Architecture-Forward Gap
framework.description: OpenID Foundation's Oct 2025 whitepaper on agent identity management is entirely forward-looking ('future challenges', 'should be avoided', 'lacks support'). Zero post-mortems, zero incident references, zero 'we tried X and it broke.' From the standards body closest to the problem, this is the strongest confirmation yet that multi-hop delegation failures aren't being published because they haven't been attempted at scale in production.
framework.raw_quote: Recursive delegation creates risks. Agents spawning sub-agents or communicating tasks to other agents create complex authorization chains without clear scope attenuation mechanisms.

source.title: [PDF] Identity Management for Agentic AI - OpenID
